segments.text: As the deer pants for the water brooks,
So pants my soul for You, O God.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: My soul thirsts for God, for the living God. When shall I come and appear before God?
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: My tears have been my food day and night,
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: While they continually say to me,

When I remember these things, I pour out my soul within me. For I used to go with the multitude; I went with them to the house of God, With the voice of joy and praise, With a multitude that kept a pilgrim feast.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Why are you cast down, O my soul? And why are you disquieted within me? Hope in God, for I shall yet praise Him For the help of His countenance.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: O my God, my soul is cast down within me; Therefore I will remember You from the land of the Jordan, And from the heights of Hermon, From the Hill Mizar.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Deep calls unto deep at the noise of Your waterfalls; All Your waves and billows have gone over me.
The Lord will command His lovingkindness in the daytime, And in the night His song shall be with me— A prayer to the God of my life.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will say to God my Rock, Why have You forgotten me? Why do I go mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: As with a breaking of my bones,
My enemies reproach me, While they say to me all day long, Where is your God?
segments.speaker: Enemies

segments.text: Why are you cast down, O my soul? And why are you disquieted within me? Hope in God; For I shall yet praise Him, The help of my countenance and my God.
segments.speaker: Psalmist
